title: Blink-Pause-Relation (Competing Speaker Paradigm)
description: This dataset comprises pooled electrooculography (EOG) data from 41 participants across two studies investigating the relationship between eye blinks and attention during a competing speaker paradigm. Participants performed selective auditory attention tasks while listening to competing audiobooks, with experimental conditions varying between studies regarding sound presentation (omnidirectional versus beamforming). The dataset integrates EOG recordings from Jaeger et al. (2020) and Holtze et al. (2021) to enable comparative analysis of oculomotor behavior during dichotic listening tasks.
methods_description: Participants (n=41; 20 from Jaeger et al. 2020, 21 from Holtze et al. 2021) performed a two-competing speaker paradigm with selective attention instructions (attend left or right audiobook). Experimental blocks consisted of 10-minute audiobook presentations. Jaeger et al. (2020) presented both audiobooks at equal loudness across six blocks. Holtze et al. (2021) alternated between omnidirectional (equal loudness) and beamforming (attended book louder) conditions across five blocks, with the first block always omnidirectional. EOG channels were recorded throughout all sessions.
